metadata.districtModel: five sections; five district slots per section; D13 is the seat of power

cityDNA.coreFantasy: A trolls city where Trollstone Bridge-Back Hall of the Fen, Nursery Basin Beacon, Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry, and the five-section district spine turn local identity into functional civic space.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Grendhollow should feel immediately identifiable through exact landmarks and materials, not through generic fantasy-city shorthand.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Grendhollow organizes power through Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry, public response through Nursery Basin Beacon, civic memory through Trollstone Bridge-Back Hall of the Fen, and daily life through S1 Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, S2 Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, S3 Records / Archives Hall, S4 Medical / Healing Civic Hall, S5 Arena / Training Civic Hall.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Cedar, Thatch, Malachite, Resin, Painted Plaster, Swamp Edge, Tidal Marsh, Reed Marsh, Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Stone Giant Memory, and exact anchors including Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry, Nursery Basin Beacon, Trollstone Bridge-Back Hall of the Fen, Training Arena, Lock House, Healing Hall, Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, Shell Inlay Monument, Spawning Pool Nook, Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A trolls civic culture where architecture, public obligations, roads, landmark approaches, and everyday work must all read as part of the same civilization.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city symbol system is built from exact names and visible functions: Trollstone Bridge-Back Hall of the Fen, Nursery Basin Beacon, Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry, and section anchors such as Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, Records / Archives Hall, Medical / Healing Civic Hall, Arena / Training Civic Hall.

canonLocks: Grendhollow uses city code GRE.
The Wonder is Trollstone Bridge-Back Hall of the Fen.
The Beacon is Nursery Basin Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry and occupies District 13.
The city uses five sections with five tactical district slots per section.

The source-backed section spine is: S1 Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, S2 Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, S3 Records / Archives Hall, S4 Medical / Healing Civic Hall, S5 Arena / Training Civic Hall.

rejectedReads: Do not treat Grendhollow as a generic fantasy settlement with renamed landmarks.
Do not collapse the five-section board into a single mood or single district.
Do not replace exact anchor names with generic summaries.
Do not invent missing street-section landmarks when a street source is absent.
cautionFlags: Keep every named Wonder, Beacon, Seat, section civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook exact when present in sidecars.
If an image appears to deviate from a sidecar, preserve the exact name but avoid overclaiming visual details not supported by the source package.
Every district asset should remain usable for narrative scenes, tactical board dioramas, and city map backgrounds.

environment.environmentalLogic: The environment should make Grendhollow's exact civic anchors plausible at street scale: terrain routes people toward section landmarks, lighting clarifies public authority, and water or landscape features remain source-backed rather than decorative filler.

civilization.governance: Government is centered on Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry. Its authority should be visible through courts, records, public access routes, civic lamps, guards or attendants, and the way citizens respond to D13.

streets.appearance: Streets should show Grendhollow's exact anchor system at pedestrian scale: S1 Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, S2 Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, S3 Records / Archives Hall, S4 Medical / Healing Civic Hall, S5 Arena / Training Civic Hall. streets.movement: Movement should be readable for tactical-board assets: citizens pass between 25 district slots, section anchors, D13, service streets, and landmark approaches without visual clutter hiding the civic purpose.
sounds: landmark-specific sounds from water, bells, animals, machines, or crowds when source-backed
smells: worn stone, timber, metal, plaster, or fabric
streets.lighting: Use the package's source-backed civic lighting, daylight/golden-hour reads, lanterns, and beacon glow. Lighting must help identify authority, safety, and landmark function.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce the local terrain and materials without overwhelming the city identity or inventing unsupported climate extremes.
streets.dayCycle: Day scenes emphasize errands, trade, records, repairs, rituals, food, and section-level movement.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes emphasize lantern routes, closed thresholds, guarded landmarks, quiet work, and beacon visibility.

common: street services tied to section anchors
exports: specialized tools or ritual goods as supported by anchors
imports: dyes, oils, spices, timber, stone, metal, glass, or cloth as needed by the architecture

housing.wealthy: Prestige housing clusters near Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry, high terraces, or landmark-facing routes, using the best source-backed materials and controlled views of civic space.
housing.middle: Shop-over-house units, courtyard homes, guild-adjacent apartments, and family rooms near section anchors.
housing.poor: Smaller back-street rooms, work-adjacent housing, shared courtyards, service alleys, patched materials, and dense but still city-specific lived-in detail.
